meta_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity project template to Solve can't collaborate (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't collaborate. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancer Guide: Solve Collaboration Issues with Antigravity
content.seo_description: Overcome project collaboration bottlenecks using the Antigravity template and Claude Code. A high-efficiency workflow for freelance developers.
content.answer_block: Collaboration silos kill freelance momentum. By standardizing your architecture with the Antigravity project template, you create a 'single source of truth' that allows Claude Code to context-load your entire stack instantly. This eliminates ambiguity, aligns your AI agents with your codebase, and lets you ship production-ready features for clients significantly faster.
content.intro:
You’ve been there: a client project spirals into a chaotic mess of fragmented Slack messages and unversioned scripts. The 'collaboration gap' isn't just a communication failure; it’s a structural one. When your project lacks a rigid, AI-readable foundation, you end up spending more time explaining context to your tools than actually building software.

It’s time to stop fighting the architecture. The Antigravity template provides a deterministic, modular skeleton designed specifically for AI-assisted development. By pairing this with Claude Code, you transform your workflow from a manual grind into an automated, high-velocity engine that keeps your clients impressed and your development cycles lean.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Foundation
steps.description: Establish the project structure to ensure all future AI interactions have perfect context mapping.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity template. Set up standard folder structure, including /src, /tests, and a structured /docs directory for technical requirements.

steps.title: Configure the Context Manifest
steps.description: Define the project constraints and coding standards to prevent code drift during rapid development.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a manifest.json file that defines our code style guide, naming conventions, and dependency constraints for this project.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create manifest.json based on project constraints'

steps.title: Implement Modular Feature Slicing
steps.description: Break down client requirements into isolated modules that Antigravity can track independently.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the user story requirements and break them into modular, decoupled feature files within the /src/modules directory.

steps.title: Automate Type-Safe Integration
steps.description: Ensure consistency across the stack by forcing strict typing within the Antigravity template parameters.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Apply strict TypeScript interfaces to all new modules created in the /src/modules directory to ensure type safety across the board.

steps.title: Sync and Validate Deployment
steps.description: Perform a final check to ensure the project is fully deployable and ready for client review.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a comprehensive audit of the project against the Antigravity build standards and identify any missing documentation or dependencies.

pro_tips: Always keep your manifest.json at the root; it acts as the 'brain' for Claude Code to understand your project's intent.
Use git hooks to trigger a Claude Code audit every time you push, ensuring no 'un-Antigravity' code enters your main branch.
If a prompt gets stuck, explicitly reference the module path in your Antigravity prompt to constrain the AI's search space.
Use custom 'agent-instructions.md' files in your template to define how you want the AI to format its code comments for client-facing deliverables.
Treat your Antigravity template as a living document; update your base template every time you find a reusable pattern.
content.next_steps: Once your first module is deployed, run a full suite of integration tests and present the 'Project Manifest' to your client to establish professional authority.
content.conversion_block: Stop struggling with fragmented workflows and start building with professional-grade precision. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
